{{commons category|Category:Sakahogi-jinja}} [[JA:坂祝神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11425559}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine | Name = Sakahogi Shrine | Image = [[Image:Sakahogi Jinja2008-2.jpg|270px]]
Torii gate | Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Sakahogi Town|Q598446|WD=Q598446|1=Sakahogi, Gifu}} Sakakura, Aza Kamoyama 1507-2 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Masakatsuyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | Founded = Before the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} era | Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | Annual festival = First Saturday of April (Shiraku-sai), Sunday (Honraku-sai) | Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Sakahogi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sakahogi Shrine''' (さかほぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Sakahogi Town|Q598446|WD=Q598446|1=Sakahogi, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is listed as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shrine recorded in the Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. The name of Sakahogi Town is derived from this shrine. Sakahogi Shrine, {{ill|lt=Agatanushi Shrine (Minokamo City)|Q11608132|WD=Q11608132|lt=Agatanushi Shrine|1=Agatanushi Shrine (Minokamo)}} (Ōta Town, {{ill|lt=Minokamo City|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}}), and {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Minokamo City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamo Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Minokamo City|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}}) are collectively referred to as the '''Three Kamo Shrines'''. === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown. {{Citation needed|However, since the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mino no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=UNKNOWN}} were imperial descendants following {{ill|lt=Hikozai-no-Ō|Q11487910|WD=Q11487910|1=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi}}, there is a theory that the founding of Sakahogi Shrine dates back even earlier than the Heian period, when devotion to Kamo shrines flourished.|date=September 2020}} In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, Sakahogi was reportedly pronounced “Sakahafuri.” {{Citation needed|According to Abe Einosuke’s *Nōhi Ryōkoku Tsūshi*, because the shrine’s name was Kamo Myōjin, when considered together with the enshrined deities of Kyoto’s {{ill|lt=Kamigamo Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Shimogamo Shrine|Q701620|WD=Q701620|lt=Kamo Mioya Shrine|1=Shimogamo Shrine}}, the enshrined deities of Sakahogi Shrine are presumed to be the same as those of Shimogamo Shrine: “{{ill|lt=Tamayorihime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tadasu Tamayorihime no Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}},” and her father deity, “{{ill|lt=Kamo no Taketsunumi no Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}}.” There is also a theory that the Shikinaisha Sakahogi Shrine corresponds to the Hōri-be Shrine listed in the register, and that Sakahogi was also called Hōri-be. If this assumption is correct, Hōri-be would be the older term, and those Hōri-be may have been ritualists serving the nearby Agatanushi Shrine, or alternatively members of an immigrant clan from Wu listed in the Shinsen Shōjiroku. The former seems more plausible. In that case, it could also be inferred that the original deity of Sakahogi Shrine was the ancestral deity of the Hōri-be, who were closely connected to the Kamo Agata clan—descendants of Hikozai-no-Ō, the enshrined deity of Agatanushi Shrine.|date=September 2020}}. In {{ill|lt=1062|Q19396|WD=Q19396|1=1062}} ({{ill|lt=Kōhei|Q1193572|WD=Q1193572|1=Kōhei}} 5), during the {{ill|lt=Earlier Nine Years’ War|Q1187448|WD=Q1187448|1=Former Nine Years' War}}, {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|Q11564276|WD=Q11564276|lt=Kamo Jirō Yoshitsuna|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsuna}} stopped at Sakahogi Shrine on his way to {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}} as reinforcements for his elder brother {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613|WD=Q32613|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, and prayed for victory. One theory holds that Kamo Jirō felt a connection because Sakahogi Shrine stands on Mount Kamo. On his return from Mutsu Province, he is said to have stopped by again and constructed shrine buildings. In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6), it became a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} of Gifu Prefecture’s 10th large district, 11th small district. On March 27, {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40), it was designated by the district head as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku. In {{ill|lt=1912|Q2077|WD=Q2077|1=1912}} ({{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 1), the shrine buildings were damaged by a severe storm. The current buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083|1=1914}} (Taishō 3). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Masakayama-tsumi-no-Kami|Q109368726|WD=Q109368726|lt=Masakatsuyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Masakatsuyamatsumi-no-Kami}} (Masakayama-tsumi-no-Kami) According to the *Kojiki*, when Izanagi slew Kagutsuchi (軻遇突智), the Eight Mountain Deities were born from Kagutsuchi’s body. This deity was born from Kagutsuchi’s head. === Subsidiary shrines === Hakusan Shrine, Tsushima Shrine, Mikuwashi Shrine, Inari Shrine, Sarutahiko Shrine, Yamagami Shrine === Location === * 1507-2 Sakakura, Aza Kamoyama, Sakahogi Town, Kamo District, Gifu Prefecture === Access === {{commonscat|Sakahogi-jinja}} * About 2 km from {{ill|lt=Sakahogi Station|Q873142|WD=Q873142|1=Sakahogi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}} === References === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakahogi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakahogi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:坂祝町の建築物]] [[Category:坂祝町の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]]

It is listed as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shrine recorded in the Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. The name of Sakahogi Town is derived from this shrine. Sakahogi Shrine, {{ill|lt=Agatanushi Shrine (Minokamo City)|Q11608132|WD=Q11608132|lt=Agatanushi Shrine|1=Agatanushi Shrine (Minokamo)}} (Ōta Town, {{ill|lt=Minokamo City|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}}), and {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Minokamo City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamo Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Minokamo City|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}}) are collectively referred to as the '''Three Kamo Shrines'''. === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown. {{Citation needed|However, since the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mino no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=UNKNOWN}} were imperial descendants following {{ill|lt=Hikozai-no-Ō|Q11487910|WD=Q11487910|1=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi}}, there is a theory that the founding of Sakahogi Shrine dates back even earlier than the Heian period, when devotion to Kamo shrines flourished.|date=September 2020}} In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, Sakahogi was reportedly pronounced “Sakahafuri.” {{Citation needed|According to Abe Einosuke’s *Nōhi Ryōkoku Tsūshi*, because the shrine’s name was Kamo Myōjin, when considered together with the enshrined deities of Kyoto’s {{ill|lt=Kamigamo Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Shimogamo Shrine|Q701620|WD=Q701620|lt=Kamo Mioya Shrine|1=Shimogamo Shrine}}, the enshrined deities of Sakahogi Shrine are presumed to be the same as those of Shimogamo Shrine: “{{ill|lt=Tamayorihime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tadasu Tamayorihime no Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}},” and her father deity, “{{ill|lt=Kamo no Taketsunumi no Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}}.” There is also a theory that the Shikinaisha Sakahogi Shrine corresponds to the Hōri-be Shrine listed in the register, and that Sakahogi was also called Hōri-be. If this assumption is correct, Hōri-be would be the older term, and those Hōri-be may have been ritualists serving the nearby Agatanushi Shrine, or alternatively members of an immigrant clan from Wu listed in the Shinsen Shōjiroku. The former seems more plausible. In that case, it could also be inferred that the original deity of Sakahogi Shrine was the ancestral deity of the Hōri-be, who were closely connected to the Kamo Agata clan—descendants of Hikozai-no-Ō, the enshrined deity of Agatanushi Shrine.|date=September 2020}}. In {{ill|lt=1062|Q19396|WD=Q19396|1=1062}} ({{ill|lt=Kōhei|Q1193572|WD=Q1193572|1=Kōhei}} 5), during the {{ill|lt=Earlier Nine Years’ War|Q1187448|WD=Q1187448|1=Former Nine Years' War}}, {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|Q11564276|WD=Q11564276|lt=Kamo Jirō Yoshitsuna|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsuna}} stopped at Sakahogi Shrine on his way to {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}} as reinforcements for his elder brother {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613|WD=Q32613|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, and prayed for victory. One theory holds that Kamo Jirō felt a connection because Sakahogi Shrine stands on Mount Kamo. On his return from Mutsu Province, he is said to have stopped by again and constructed shrine buildings. In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6), it became a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} of Gifu Prefecture’s 10th large district, 11th small district. On March 27, {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40), it was designated by the district head as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku. In {{ill|lt=1912|Q2077|WD=Q2077|1=1912}} ({{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 1), the shrine buildings were damaged by a severe storm. The current buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083|1=1914}} (Taishō 3). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Masakayama-tsumi-no-Kami|Q109368726|WD=Q109368726|lt=Masakatsuyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Masakatsuyamatsumi-no-Kami}} (Masakayama-tsumi-no-Kami) According to the *Kojiki*, when Izanagi slew Kagutsuchi (軻遇突智), the Eight Mountain Deities were born from Kagutsuchi’s body. This deity was born from Kagutsuchi’s head. === Subsidiary shrines === Hakusan Shrine, Tsushima Shrine, Mikuwashi Shrine, Inari Shrine, Sarutahiko Shrine, Yamagami Shrine === Location === * 1507-2 Sakakura, Aza Kamoyama, Sakahogi Town, Kamo District, Gifu Prefecture === Access === {{commonscat|Sakahogi-jinja}} * About 2 km from {{ill|lt=Sakahogi Station|Q873142|WD=Q873142|1=Sakahogi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}} === References === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakahogi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakahogi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:坂祝町の建築物]] [[Category:坂祝町の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]]

If this assumption is correct, Hōri-be would be the older term, and those Hōri-be may have been ritualists serving the nearby Agatanushi Shrine, or alternatively members of an immigrant clan from Wu listed in the Shinsen Shōjiroku. The former seems more plausible. In that case, it could also be inferred that the original deity of Sakahogi Shrine was the ancestral deity of the Hōri-be, who were closely connected to the Kamo Agata clan—descendants of Hikozai-no-Ō, the enshrined deity of Agatanushi Shrine.|date=September 2020}}. In {{ill|lt=1062|Q19396|WD=Q19396|1=1062}} ({{ill|lt=Kōhei|Q1193572|WD=Q1193572|1=Kōhei}} 5), during the {{ill|lt=Earlier Nine Years’ War|Q1187448|WD=Q1187448|1=Former Nine Years' War}}, {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|Q11564276|WD=Q11564276|lt=Kamo Jirō Yoshitsuna|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsuna}} stopped at Sakahogi Shrine on his way to {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}} as reinforcements for his elder brother {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613|WD=Q32613|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, and prayed for victory. One theory holds that Kamo Jirō felt a connection because Sakahogi Shrine stands on Mount Kamo. On his return from Mutsu Province, he is said to have stopped by again and constructed shrine buildings. In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6), it became a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} of Gifu Prefecture’s 10th large district, 11th small district. In {{ill|lt=1912|Q2077|WD=Q2077|1=1912}} ({{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 1), the shrine buildings were damaged by a severe storm. The current buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083|1=1914}} (Taishō 3). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Masakayama-tsumi-no-Kami|Q109368726|WD=Q109368726|lt=Masakatsuyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Masakatsuyamatsumi-no-Kami}} (Masakayama-tsumi-no-Kami) According to the *Kojiki*, when Izanagi slew Kagutsuchi (軻遇突智), the Eight Mountain Deities were born from Kagutsuchi’s body. This deity was born from Kagutsuchi’s head. === Subsidiary shrines === Hakusan Shrine, Tsushima Shrine, Mikuwashi Shrine, Inari Shrine, Sarutahiko Shrine, Yamagami Shrine === Location === * 1507-2 Sakakura, Aza Kamoyama, Sakahogi Town, Kamo District, Gifu Prefecture === Access === {{commonscat|Sakahogi-jinja}} * About 2 km from {{ill|lt=Sakahogi Station|Q873142|WD=Q873142|1=Sakahogi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}} === References === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakahogi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakahogi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:坂祝町の建築物]] [[Category:坂祝町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]]

{{commons category|Category:Sakahogi-jinja}} [[JA:坂祝神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11425559}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine | Name = Sakahogi Shrine | Image = [[Image:Sakahogi Jinja2008-2.jpg|270px]]
Torii gate | Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Sakahogi Town|Q598446|WD=Q598446|1=Sakahogi, Gifu}} Sakakura, Aza Kamoyama 1507-2 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Masakatsuyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | Founded = Before the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} era | Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | Annual festival = First Saturday of April (Shiraku-sai), Sunday (Honraku-sai) | Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Sakahogi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sakahogi Shrine''' (さかほぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Sakahogi Town|Q598446|WD=Q598446|1=Sakahogi, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is listed as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shrine recorded in the Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. The name of Sakahogi Town is derived from this shrine. Sakahogi Shrine, {{ill|lt=Agatanushi Shrine (Minokamo City)|Q11608132|WD=Q11608132|lt=Agatanushi Shrine|1=Agatanushi Shrine (Minokamo)}} (Ōta Town, {{ill|lt=Minokamo City|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}}), and {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Minokamo City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamo Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Minokamo City|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}}) are collectively referred to as the '''Three Kamo Shrines'''. === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown. {{Citation needed|However, since the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mino no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=UNKNOWN}} were imperial descendants following {{ill|lt=Hikozai-no-Ō|Q11487910|WD=Q11487910|1=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi}}, there is a theory that the founding of Sakahogi Shrine dates back even earlier than the Heian period, when devotion to Kamo shrines flourished.|date=September 2020}} In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, Sakahogi was reportedly pronounced “Sakahafuri.” {{Citation needed|According to Abe Einosuke’s *Nōhi Ryōkoku Tsūshi*, because the shrine’s name was Kamo Myōjin, when considered together with the enshrined deities of Kyoto’s {{ill|lt=Kamigamo Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Shimogamo Shrine|Q701620|WD=Q701620|lt=Kamo Mioya Shrine|1=Shimogamo Shrine}}, the enshrined deities of Sakahogi Shrine are presumed to be the same as those of Shimogamo Shrine: “{{ill|lt=Tamayorihime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tadasu Tamayorihime no Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}},” and her father deity, “{{ill|lt=Kamo no Taketsunumi no Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}}.” There is also a theory that the Shikinaisha Sakahogi Shrine corresponds to the Hōri-be Shrine listed in the register, and that Sakahogi was also called Hōri-be. If this assumption is correct, Hōri-be would be the older term, and those Hōri-be may have been ritualists serving the nearby Agatanushi Shrine, or alternatively members of an immigrant clan from Wu listed in the Shinsen Shōjiroku. The former seems more plausible. In that case, it could also be inferred that the original deity of Sakahogi Shrine was the ancestral deity of the Hōri-be, who were closely connected to the Kamo Agata clan—descendants of Hikozai-no-Ō, the enshrined deity of Agatanushi Shrine.|date=September 2020}}. In {{ill|lt=1062|Q19396|WD=Q19396|1=1062}} ({{ill|lt=Kōhei|Q1193572|WD=Q1193572|1=Kōhei}} 5), during the {{ill|lt=Earlier Nine Years’ War|Q1187448|WD=Q1187448|1=Former Nine Years' War}}, {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|Q11564276|WD=Q11564276|lt=Kamo Jirō Yoshitsuna|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsuna}} stopped at Sakahogi Shrine on his way to {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}} as reinforcements for his elder brother {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613|WD=Q32613|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, and prayed for victory. One theory holds that Kamo Jirō felt a connection because Sakahogi Shrine stands on Mount Kamo. On his return from Mutsu Province, he is said to have stopped by again and constructed shrine buildings. In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6), it became a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} of Gifu Prefecture’s 10th large district, 11th small district. On March 27, {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40), it was designated by the district head as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku. In {{ill|lt=1912|Q2077|WD=Q2077|1=1912}} ({{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 1), the shrine buildings were damaged by a severe storm. The current buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083|1=1914}} (Taishō 3). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Masakayama-tsumi-no-Kami|Q109368726|WD=Q109368726|lt=Masakatsuyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Masakatsuyamatsumi-no-Kami}} (Masakayama-tsumi-no-Kami) According to the *Kojiki*, when Izanagi slew Kagutsuchi (軻遇突智), the Eight Mountain Deities were born from Kagutsuchi’s body. This deity was born from Kagutsuchi’s head. === Subsidiary shrines === Hakusan Shrine, Tsushima Shrine, Mikuwashi Shrine, Inari Shrine, Sarutahiko Shrine, Yamagami Shrine === Location === * 1507-2 Sakakura, Aza Kamoyama, Sakahogi Town, Kamo District, Gifu Prefecture === Access === {{commonscat|Sakahogi-jinja}} * About 2 km from {{ill|lt=Sakahogi Station|Q873142|WD=Q873142|1=Sakahogi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}} === References === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakahogi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakahogi Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:坂祝町の建築物]] [[Category:坂祝町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]]

In {{ill|lt=1912|Q2077|WD=Q2077|1=1912}} ({{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 1), the shrine buildings were damaged by a severe storm. The current buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083|1=1914}} (Taishō 3). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Masakayama-tsumi-no-Kami|Q109368726|WD=Q109368726|lt=Masakatsuyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Masakatsuyamatsumi-no-Kami}} (Masakayama-tsumi-no-Kami) According to the *Kojiki*, when Izanagi slew Kagutsuchi (軻遇突智), the Eight Mountain Deities were born from Kagutsuchi’s body. This deity was born from Kagutsuchi’s head. === Subsidiary shrines === Hakusan Shrine, Tsushima Shrine, Mikuwashi Shrine, Inari Shrine, Sarutahiko Shrine, Yamagami Shrine === Location === * 1507-2 Sakakura, Aza Kamoyama, Sakahogi Town, Kamo District, Gifu Prefecture === Access === {{commonscat|Sakahogi-jinja}} * About 2 km from {{ill|lt=Sakahogi Station|Q873142|WD=Q873142|1=Sakahogi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}} === References === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakahogi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakahogi Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakahogi]] [[Category:坂祝町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]]

{{commons category|Category:Sakahogi-jinja}} [[JA:坂祝神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11425559}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine | Name = Sakahogi Shrine | Image = [[Image:Sakahogi Jinja2008-2.jpg|270px]]
Torii gate | Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Sakahogi Town|Q598446|WD=Q598446|1=Sakahogi, Gifu}} Sakakura, Aza Kamoyama 1507-2 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Masakatsuyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | Founded = Before the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} era | Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | Annual festival = First Saturday of April (Shiraku-sai), Sunday (Honraku-sai) | Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Sakahogi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Sakahogi Shrine''' (さかほぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Sakahogi Town|Q598446|WD=Q598446|1=Sakahogi, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is listed as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shrine recorded in the Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. The name of Sakahogi Town is derived from this shrine. Sakahogi Shrine, {{ill|lt=Agatanushi Shrine (Minokamo City)|Q11608132|WD=Q11608132|lt=Agatanushi Shrine|1=Agatanushi Shrine (Minokamo)}} (Ōta Town, {{ill|lt=Minokamo City|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}}), and {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Minokamo City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamo Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Minokamo City|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}}) are collectively referred to as the '''Three Kamo Shrines'''. === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown. {{Citation needed|However, since the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mino no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=UNKNOWN}} were imperial descendants following {{ill|lt=Hikozai-no-Ō|Q11487910|WD=Q11487910|1=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi}}, there is a theory that the founding of Sakahogi Shrine dates back even earlier than the Heian period, when devotion to Kamo shrines flourished.|date=September 2020}} In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, Sakahogi was reportedly pronounced “Sakahafuri.” {{Citation needed|According to Abe Einosuke’s *Nōhi Ryōkoku Tsūshi*, because the shrine’s name was Kamo Myōjin, when considered together with the enshrined deities of Kyoto’s {{ill|lt=Kamigamo Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Shimogamo Shrine|Q701620|WD=Q701620|lt=Kamo Mioya Shrine|1=Shimogamo Shrine}}, the enshrined deities of Sakahogi Shrine are presumed to be the same as those of Shimogamo Shrine: “{{ill|lt=Tamayorihime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tadasu Tamayorihime no Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}},” and her father deity, “{{ill|lt=Kamo no Taketsunumi no Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}}.” There is also a theory that the Shikinaisha Sakahogi Shrine corresponds to the Hōri-be Shrine listed in the register, and that Sakahogi was also called Hōri-be. If this assumption is correct, Hōri-be would be the older term, and those Hōri-be may have been ritualists serving the nearby Agatanushi Shrine, or alternatively members of an immigrant clan from Wu listed in the Shinsen Shōjiroku. The former seems more plausible. In that case, it could also be inferred that the original deity of Sakahogi Shrine was the ancestral deity of the Hōri-be, who were closely connected to the Kamo Agata clan—descendants of Hikozai-no-Ō, the enshrined deity of Agatanushi Shrine.|date=September 2020}}. In {{ill|lt=1062|Q19396|WD=Q19396|1=1062}} ({{ill|lt=Kōhei|Q1193572|WD=Q1193572|1=Kōhei}} 5), during the {{ill|lt=Earlier Nine Years’ War|Q1187448|WD=Q1187448|1=Former Nine Years' War}}, {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|Q11564276|WD=Q11564276|lt=Kamo Jirō Yoshitsuna|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsuna}} stopped at Sakahogi Shrine on his way to {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}} as reinforcements for his elder brother {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613|WD=Q32613|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, and prayed for victory. One theory holds that Kamo Jirō felt a connection because Sakahogi Shrine stands on Mount Kamo. On his return from Mutsu Province, he is said to have stopped by again and constructed shrine buildings. In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6), it became a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} of Gifu Prefecture’s 10th large district, 11th small district. On March 27, {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40), it was designated by the district head as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku. In {{ill|lt=1912|Q2077|WD=Q2077|1=1912}} ({{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 1), the shrine buildings were damaged by a severe storm. The current buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083|1=1914}} (Taishō 3). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Masakayama-tsumi-no-Kami|Q109368726|WD=Q109368726|lt=Masakatsuyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Masakatsuyamatsumi-no-Kami}} (Masakayama-tsumi-no-Kami) According to the *Kojiki*, when Izanagi slew Kagutsuchi (軻遇突智), the Eight Mountain Deities were born from Kagutsuchi’s body. This deity was born from Kagutsuchi’s head. === Subsidiary shrines === Hakusan Shrine, Tsushima Shrine, Mikuwashi Shrine, Inari Shrine, Sarutahiko Shrine, Yamagami Shrine === Location === * 1507-2 Sakakura, Aza Kamoyama, Sakahogi Town, Kamo District, Gifu Prefecture === Access === {{commonscat|Sakahogi-jinja}} * About 2 km from {{ill|lt=Sakahogi Station|Q873142|WD=Q873142|1=Sakahogi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}} === References === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakahogi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakahogi Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakahogi]] [[Category:History of Sakahogi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]]

It is listed as a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shrine recorded in the Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. The name of Sakahogi Town is derived from this shrine. Sakahogi Shrine, {{ill|lt=Agatanushi Shrine (Minokamo City)|Q11608132|WD=Q11608132|lt=Agatanushi Shrine|1=Agatanushi Shrine (Minokamo)}} (Ōta Town, {{ill|lt=Minokamo City|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}}), and {{ill|lt=Kamo Shrine (Minokamo City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamo Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Minokamo City|Q857305|WD=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}}) are collectively referred to as the '''Three Kamo Shrines'''. === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown. {{Citation needed|However, since the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mino no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=UNKNOWN}} were imperial descendants following {{ill|lt=Hikozai-no-Ō|Q11487910|WD=Q11487910|1=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi}}, there is a theory that the founding of Sakahogi Shrine dates back even earlier than the Heian period, when devotion to Kamo shrines flourished.|date=September 2020}} In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, Sakahogi was reportedly pronounced “Sakahafuri.” {{Citation needed|According to Abe Einosuke’s *Nōhi Ryōkoku Tsūshi*, because the shrine’s name was Kamo Myōjin, when considered together with the enshrined deities of Kyoto’s {{ill|lt=Kamigamo Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi Shrine|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Shimogamo Shrine|Q701620|WD=Q701620|lt=Kamo Mioya Shrine|1=Shimogamo Shrine}}, the enshrined deities of Sakahogi Shrine are presumed to be the same as those of Shimogamo Shrine: “{{ill|lt=Tamayorihime|Q56344982|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tadasu Tamayorihime no Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}},” and her father deity, “{{ill|lt=Kamo no Taketsunumi no Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}}.” There is also a theory that the Shikinaisha Sakahogi Shrine corresponds to the Hōri-be Shrine listed in the register, and that Sakahogi was also called Hōri-be. If this assumption is correct, Hōri-be would be the older term, and those Hōri-be may have been ritualists serving the nearby Agatanushi Shrine, or alternatively members of an immigrant clan from Wu listed in the Shinsen Shōjiroku. The former seems more plausible. In that case, it could also be inferred that the original deity of Sakahogi Shrine was the ancestral deity of the Hōri-be, who were closely connected to the Kamo Agata clan—descendants of Hikozai-no-Ō, the enshrined deity of Agatanushi Shrine.|date=September 2020}}. In {{ill|lt=1062|Q19396|WD=Q19396|1=1062}} ({{ill|lt=Kōhei|Q1193572|WD=Q1193572|1=Kōhei}} 5), during the {{ill|lt=Earlier Nine Years’ War|Q1187448|WD=Q1187448|1=Former Nine Years' War}}, {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsuna|Q11564276|WD=Q11564276|lt=Kamo Jirō Yoshitsuna|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsuna}} stopped at Sakahogi Shrine on his way to {{ill|lt=Mutsu Province|Q907389|WD=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}} as reinforcements for his elder brother {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613|WD=Q32613|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, and prayed for victory. One theory holds that Kamo Jirō felt a connection because Sakahogi Shrine stands on Mount Kamo. On his return from Mutsu Province, he is said to have stopped by again and constructed shrine buildings. In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6), it became a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} of Gifu Prefecture’s 10th large district, 11th small district. On March 27, {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40), it was designated by the district head as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku. In {{ill|lt=1912|Q2077|WD=Q2077|1=1912}} ({{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 1), the shrine buildings were damaged by a severe storm. The current buildings were reconstructed in {{ill|lt=1914|Q2083|WD=Q2083|1=1914}} (Taishō 3). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Masakayama-tsumi-no-Kami|Q109368726|WD=Q109368726|lt=Masakatsuyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Masakatsuyamatsumi-no-Kami}} (Masakayama-tsumi-no-Kami) According to the *Kojiki*, when Izanagi slew Kagutsuchi (軻遇突智), the Eight Mountain Deities were born from Kagutsuchi’s body. This deity was born from Kagutsuchi’s head. === Subsidiary shrines === Hakusan Shrine, Tsushima Shrine, Mikuwashi Shrine, Inari Shrine, Sarutahiko Shrine, Yamagami Shrine === Location === * 1507-2 Sakakura, Aza Kamoyama, Sakahogi Town, Kamo District, Gifu Prefecture === Access === {{commonscat|Sakahogi-jinja}} * About 2 km from {{ill|lt=Sakahogi Station|Q873142|WD=Q873142|1=Sakahogi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}} === References === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakahogi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakahogi Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakahogi]] [[Category:History of Sakahogi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]]
